hero.question: Will Connecticut win the 2026 NCAA Tournament?
hero.prob_take: At 5.5%, the market is pricing Connecticut as a lottery ticket with worse odds than hitting a parlay, which means bettors have essentially written off a tournament run before conference play even starts. The Huskies made the Elite Eight last year and return their best players, but the market's harsh discount might be overweighting the gap between last season's success and next year's field rather than giving credit for continuity and tournament experience.

daily_take.headline: Connecticut's 5.5% Tournament Odds Are Leaving Money on the Table
daily_take.deck: Connecticut sits at 5.5% to win the 2026 NCAA Tournament, up a tenth of a point on modest volume. The market is pricing them like a mid-major when their roster construction and coaching suggest mid-seed potential. Smart money sees value here: the Big East's best team is underpriced relative to Arizona and other tournament favorites.

sidebar.headline: Israel-Yemen strike odds jumped 4 full points in 24 hours, signaling renewed escalation expectations that markets weren't pricing in yesterday.
sidebar.label: Israel Strike by March 31: 34.0%

sidebar.headline: Arizona's 36.2% tournament odds moved up 2.2 points, but Connecticut's price didn't follow proportionally, creating a relative value mismatch between similar-tier teams.
sidebar.label: Arizona 2026 Tournament: 36.2%

sidebar.headline: Trump removal odds are frozen at 6.5% on negligible volume, meaning the market either nailed it or nobody's paying attention to this liquidity trap.
sidebar.label: Trump Out by August 1: 6.5%
